title: Add manual difficulty selector (Slow / Medium / Fast) to falling-words game

requirements.text: Add a difficulty selector UI to the game's start/idle screen that lets the player choose between three word-speed presets before a session begins: Slow, Medium, and Fast. The selector should be displayed as neon-styled toggle buttons matching the existing HUD aesthetic. The currently selected difficulty should be visually highlighted (e.g., neon glow border or accent color). Default selection should be Medium.

quotes: add difficulty levels (slow, medium, fast word speed) to make it more challenging

acceptanceCriteria: Three difficulty buttons — Slow, Medium, Fast — are visible on the pre-game or idle screen.
Buttons match the neon/dark gaming aesthetic of the existing HUD.
The active/selected difficulty is visually distinct from the others (highlighted).
Medium is selected by default when the game loads or restarts.
The selector is not shown while a game session is in progress (or is disabled/locked).

requirements.text: Implement the Slow, Medium, and Fast difficulty presets by defining distinct base word fall speeds and spawn intervals for each. Slow: slower fall speed and lower spawn rate than the current default. Medium: matches approximately the current default behavior. Fast: faster fall speed and higher spawn rate than the current default. The automatic difficulty scaling that increases speed over time should still apply on top of the chosen base preset, so each difficulty level starts from a different baseline and escalates from there.

quotes: add difficulty levels (slow, medium, fast word speed) to make it more challenging

acceptanceCriteria: Selecting Slow starts words falling noticeably slower and spawning less frequently than Medium.
Selecting Medium produces behavior equivalent to the current game defaults.
Selecting Fast starts words falling noticeably faster and spawning more frequently than Medium.
The existing automatic difficulty scaling (speed and spawn rate increasing over time) still functions on top of the chosen base preset.
Switching difficulty resets the game to the initial state for that preset.

requirements.text: Display the currently active difficulty preset label (e.g., 'SLOW', 'MEDIUM', or 'FAST') in the game HUD while a session is in progress, alongside the existing HUD panels (Score, Lives, WPM, Difficulty Level). This replaces or augments the existing difficulty-level/speed-tier indicator already in the HUD.

quotes: add difficulty levels (slow, medium, fast word speed)

acceptanceCriteria: A HUD panel shows the chosen difficulty preset label during an active game session.
The label uses neon styling consistent with the rest of the HUD.
The label updates correctly if the player restarts with a different difficulty.

constraints: Do not re-introduce any timer or duration-selector UI elements.
Game over condition must remain lives-based only — no time limit.
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Preserve all existing HUD panels (Score, Streak, Lives, WPM, Difficulty tier).
nonGoals: Adding new difficulty tiers beyond Slow, Medium, and Fast.
Persisting the player's difficulty preference across sessions.
Changing the word lists or tiering system beyond adjusting fall speed and spawn rate.
Adding sound effects or new visual themes.
